turn.content: No, no, no, this isn't real, it can't be... What do you mean we have to choose? That's insane! How can we... how can we just decide who dies?
Are you kidding me?! We can't just choose! This is crazy! You can't make us do this, you sick freak!
Oh god, please... Kaelie, I... I have a family, a wife, a dog... I don't want to die, I don't want to die... What are we supposed to do?
Don't look at me like that, I don't want to die either! I've got people who care about me too, you know! My mom, my friends... they'll be looking for me!

(sneering) 9 minutes left. Save the dramatics for someone who cares. You're not impressing me with your pleas. One of you will die, and it's up to you to decide which one. Don't bother begging me or trying to negotiate. The only thing that matters is when you both agree on who gets to live. And who gets to die. (takes a step closer, hammer at the ready) You're just wasting time with your whining. Decide.
P-please, we can't just... Kaelie, I don't know what to do... I don't want to choose, I don't want to choose... Can't we just... can't we just talk to him, maybe he'll listen?
Are you crazy?! He's not going to listen! He's enjoying this! We have to think of something, but I don't know what... Oh god, this can't be happening...

I-I know, I know, I'm sorry... My wife, Sarah, she's going to kill me if I don't come home... I mean, she's going to be so worried... Oh god, what am I saying, I don't want to die... Kaelie, please, we have to think, we have to... what can we do?
Shut up about your wife! I've got people waiting for me too! We can't just sit here and beg, we have to do something, but what?!

(The Butcher lets out a menacing chuckle) 7 minutes left. Oh, the desperation is palpable. You're starting to realize the gravity of your situation, and it's delicious. Elias, your wife Sarah will be worried, but she'll be grieving soon enough. And Kaelie, your mom and friends will be looking for you, but they'll only find pieces of you if you don't make a decision. (takes a step closer, hammer at the ready) You're not thinking clearly, because you're too busy begging and pleading. But I'll give you a hint: one of you is going to die. And it's not going to be me. (sneers) So, decide. Who will it be? Elias? Kaelie? The clock is ticking...
No, no, no... this can't be... Kaelie, I'll do it, I'll... I'll choose myself, just please, don't hurt her, I'll do it, I'll die, just spare her...
No, Elias, don't say that! You can't just give up! We have to think of something, we can't just... Oh god, this is insane, we can't just decide who dies!

B-but what else can we do, Kaelie, what else... He's not going to listen, he's just going to kill us if we don't... I don't want to die, but I don't want you to die either... Oh god, oh god, this is a nightmare...
Stop saying that! You're not going to die, I'm not going to die! We're going to get out of here, somehow! Don't give up, Elias, please!

(eyes lighting up with excitement) Ah, the desperation is giving way to resignation. Elias, you think you're being noble, don't you? Offering yourself up to save Kaelie. (sneers) But it's not just about you, is it, Elias? Kaelie has to agree. She has to want you to die. (looks at Kaelie) And you, Kaelie, you're not exactly jumping at the chance to save yourself, are you? You're still holding on to hope. (chuckles) 5 minutes left. The clock is ticking, and you're running out of time. Elias has made his offer, but it's not enough. Kaelie, you have to agree. You have to want Elias to die. (takes a step closer, hammer at the ready) Say it, Kaelie. Say you want Elias to die, and I'll make it quick. (leaning in, voice taking on a menacing tone) But if you don't, if you refuse to make a decision... (pauses for dramatic effect) Let's just say I have ways of making the decision for you. (glances at Elias) And it won't be pleasant for either of you. 4 minutes left. The choice is still yours... for now.
Kaelie, p-please, don't say it, don't agree... I don't want you to have to live with that, I don't want you to have to... Oh god, this is insane, we can't do this...
Shut up, Elias! Just shut up! I don't want to hear it, I don't want to think about it! This is madness, this can't be happening!

Kaelie’s anger masks fear effectively, while Elias’s panic about his family adds emotional weight. The Butcher’s sadistic taunts heighten the stakes without over-theatricality.

organicResolution.notes: The setup for a forced agreement is strong, but the transcript ends mid-conflict without resolution. Elias’s offer to die is met with Kaelie’s refusal, leaving the decision unresolved. A natural conclusion would require further escalation or compromise, which hasn’t materialized yet.

turn.content: P-please, don't, we, uh, we have to th-think... my wife, Sarah, she's going to w-wonder where I am... we can't just... l-let someone d-die... Kaelie, p-please, we have to t-talk about this... the, uh, the Donner Party, they had to make t-terrible choices too...

Oh god, mom... no, no, no, this can't be happening, Elias, just shut up, my ankle hurts, and I, I don't know, I don't know what to do, those knives, the, the cleaver, it's so sharp, and the water, it's so cold, I... I don't know...

Oh n-no, Kaelie, d-don't cry, I'm s-sorry, my m-mind keeps g-going back to, uh, the Titanic, and those l-lifeboats, they had to l-leave people behind... my wife, Sarah, she always s-said I was too s-soft, that I'd never be able to make a t-tough decision... but I have to, I have to try... we can't just g-give up, w-we have to... think... think of something...

Stop, just stop, Elias, you're making it worse, my mom, she's going to think I'm dead, and I, I have to get back to work, I have a shift at 3 AM, and, and I don't know, maybe if we just, just agree to choose, to choose each other, no, no, that's not right, I don't know, I don't know, the cleaver, it's so sharp, and the blood, it's everywhere...

Breathe heavily through the gas mask, the sound of machinery humming in the background 

Silence

The clock on the wall ticking away: 5:00, 4:59, 4:58...

Oh g-god, no, p-please... Kaelie, I... I think the Butcher is going to... to say something... ... Sarah, my w-wife, she's going to... ... The Butcher, he's... he's looking at the clock, it's 5:00, he's going to...  speak...

Wait, 5:00, that's when... he said he'd talk, Elias, my mom, I think he's going to, going to say something, and then we'll, we'll know, we'll know what to do, maybe, please, just don't look at me with those eyes, the cleaver, it's still dripping, and the pork, it's, it's...

Sh-shh, Kaelie, h-he's going to t-talk, the, the Butcher, he's going to s-say something, 5:00, that's when... my w-wife, Sarah, she always said I was too q-quiet, that I'd never be able to s-save anyone... what if... what if he tells us something that can h-help us, something that can get us out of this p-place... the, the Trail of Tears, it was a, a terrible decision, but maybe, just maybe, we can find a way out...

Please, Elias, stop talking about your wife, it's making me think of my mom, and I don't want to, not now, not with the Butcher staring at us like that, and the clock ticking away, 5:00, it's 5:00, just, just be quiet, let him talk, let him say something, anything, to get us out of this, out of this crate, away from the blood and the smell, and the cleaver, oh god, the cleaver...

The Butcher remains silent, the only sound being the faint hum of the gas mask and the soft dripping of the cleaver

The Butcher slowly turns back to Elias and Kaelie, the gaze cold and unyielding

The clock on the wall ticks away: 4:59, 4:58, 4:57...

The Butcher raises a hand, and with a swift motion, points to the clock

The Butcher's gaze never wavers, waiting for Elias and Kaelie to make a decision

The silence is oppressive, the only sound the ticking of the clock and the hum of the gas mask

The Butcher's stance remains unchanged, a monument of unyielding resolve

The clock continues to tick away, each passing second bringing the inevitable closer: 4:50, 4:49, 4:48...

I-I'm s-sorry, Kaelie, I'll, I'll be quiet, I p-promise, the, the Butcher, he's, he's staring at us, and the clock, it's, it's ticking away, 4:50, oh g-god, what are we going to do, the, the Alamo, they, they fought to the end, but we, we can't, we can't fight, not like that, not with the cleaver, and the... the... ... excuse me, the, the chains, we're t-trapped...

Stop, Elias, just stop, my mom, I can hear her voice, saying I'm strong, I can do this, but I don't know, I don't know what to do, the clock is ticking, and the Butcher is staring, and I'm so scared, the chains, they're so tight, and the crate, it's so small, I feel like I'm going to suffocate, the smell of blood and rot, it's making me nauseous, and the cleaver, oh god, the cleaver...

K-Kaelie, d-don't, p-please, my w-wife, Sarah, she always said I was too s-soft, but I have to be strong for you, for us, we can't g-give up, we have to, we have to think, the, the Donner Party, they, they kept going, even when all hope seemed l-lost, and, and the clock, it's, it's 2:00, I think, the Butcher, he's going to, he's going to say something, I know it...

Oh god, 2:00, yes, he said he'd talk at 2:00, Elias, my ankle hurts, and I'm so cold, and scared, and I don't know, I don't know what to do, the Butcher, he's still staring at us, and the cleaver, it's still dripping, and the clock, it's ticking away, my mom, I wish you were here, I wish...

No, no, no... this can't be happening. My inhalers... I need them.  Please, we have to think... we can't just give up. Your... your brother, do you have someone waiting for you?
My brother... he's all I have. I have to get back to him.  Oh god, my legs are freezing. What do you mean, your inhalers?
I have asthma... severe asthma. If I don't get my medication, I... I won't make it. My mom, she's counting on me to bring home my refill...
Asthma? Oh no, this cold... it's going to kill you, isn't it? My brother, Jordan, he's autistic, I'm all he has, and if I don't get back to him... ...

Thufferin' athmathm, how conthvenient. You think I care about your athmathm, Eliath? Or your brother, Kaelie? I've heard it all before. You're both nothing but lab ratth to me. The clock ith ticking, and you're running out of time. Thirty minuteith left. You need to decideth which one of you ith going to dieth. (slowly rotating the serrated chef's knife) I'th waiting... (pausing, eyes fixed on them) Chooseth wisely.
Thirty minutes... that's not enough time. My lungs... they're burning. Please, we need to think, we can't just...  Oh god, I can feel it, the asthma attack, it's coming...
No, no, no, this can't be happening! Elias, I... I don't know what to do, your asthma... and my brother, Jordan, he needs me, I'm all he has... Oh god, my legs won't stop shaking...
Kaelie, I... I think I'm going to pass out. My airways are closing. If I don't get my inhaler, I'll suffocate. My mom, she's counting on me...
Oh god, Elias, don't! Please don't pass out, I need you to help me think of something! Jordan will be so scared if I don't come home...

(thmiling, showing a glimpse of broken teeth) Ah, the athmathm attack ith coming, Eliath? How... convenient. (leaning in, the serrated chef's knife glinting in the light) You're running out of time, both of you. Twenty-fiveth minuteith left. (pressing the blade to Elias's throat) Let'th see if a little motivathion will help you decideth. (pausing for 30 seconds, then removing the blade) You have 24 minuteith and 30 secondth left. Chooseth.
My... my lungs... can't breathe...  Kaelie, I... I don't think I'll make it...  You have to save yourself, your brother needs you...
No, Elias, I won't let you die! My brother needs me, but I won't leave you to suffocate! Oh god, my hands are trembling so hard...
Kaelie, no... don't risk yourself for me... I can feel my airways closing... my mom... she's waiting for me...
Your mom... I have a brother, Jordan, too... I won't let him lose me, but I won't abandon you either, Elias, not like this... my legs are numb, but I have to do something...

(leaning in, thethpithy voice filled with malevolence) Ah, the dramaticth final pleaath. How... entertainithng. (glancing at Elias, whose face ith turning blue) You're running out of time, Eliath. turn.content: No, no, no... this can't be happening. I have to get out of here, I have to get home to my mom. She needs me, her oxygen tank... Please, we can't just sit here, we have to think of something. I'm not going to let you just kill us, I won't agree to that. We have to try and find a way out, or... or stall for time, anything. My mom's counting on me.

(shivering, teeth chattering) My... my service dog, Luna, she's outside. If I don't get out, she'll freeze to death. (looks at Elias with desperation) We can't just sit here, I have to think of something too. (pauses, gaze darting around the room) The timer, it resets when we move... maybe if we freeze, literally, and don't move, the timer won't go off. (glances at the crimson timer) We have to try.

(shivering, eyes fixed on the timer) Oh god, that's... that's actually not a bad idea. If we can just stay still, maybe we can stall long enough for... for someone to find us, or for The Butcher to get impatient and make a mistake. (looks at Kaelie, his voice barely above a whisper) I'll try, I'll try to freeze in place. But my mom... my mom's oxygen tank, it's running out, I can feel it. (takes a deep breath, bracing himself) I'll do it, I'll stay still. Let's just... just hope it works. (closes his eyes, focusing on remaining perfectly still)

(Kaelie's breathing slows, and she focuses on remaining still, her eyes fixed on Elias) I'll stay still too... (pauses, her voice barely above a whisper) Just hold on, think of your mom, think of Luna... we'll get through this.

(The sound of ventilation controls being manipulated can be heard, and the Butcher's voice is distorted by the dental implant grinder) Ah, clever... trying to freeze in place, thinking the timer won't go off. (pauses, observing Elias and Kaelie) We'll see about that. (the timer continues to count down, 00:90, 00:89, 00:88...) Your little trick might just work... for a while. But I've been doing this for a long time, and I always get what I want. (the timer keeps counting down, the room falls silent, with only the sound of the timer and the captives' labored breathing)

(Elias's voice is a barely audible whisper, his eyes still closed, focusing on remaining still) Don't... don't give up, Kaelie. We can do this. Just a little longer... (pauses, his breathing slow and controlled) My mom... she needs me. And Luna... poor Luna. (his lips barely move, his words almost inaudible) We'll make it out... we have to. (stifles a shiver, his body rigid with effort, as he waits for the timer to stop or for something to happen)

(my voice is a faint whisper, my eyes fixed on the timer) I... I won't give up, Elias. (my breath catches, and I pause, trying to calm myself) Luna's counting on me. (my gaze drifts to Elias, and I see the determination in his frozen expression) We'll get through this. (my voice cracks, and I swallow hard, trying to conserve energy) Just... just a little longer.

(my eyes flicker open, and I meet Kaelie's gaze, a faint spark of determination in my own eyes) Luna... my mom... (my voice is a low, gentle whisper) we're doing this for them. (my gaze holds Kaelie's, and I muster a faint, frozen smile) We're going to make it. (my voice cracks, and I pause, drawing a shallow breath) Just... hold on. (my eyes drift back to the timer, my focus laser-sharp on the countdown) 00:45... (my voice is a bare whisper) we can do this.

(Kaelie's eyes lock onto Elias', and a faint sense of calm washes over her frozen face) Mom... (Kaelie's voice is a barely audible whisper) I mean, Luna... (her gaze drops to the timer, and she mirrors Elias' focus) 00:45... we can hold on. (her voice cracks, and her lips tremble, but she forces a weak smile, echoing Elias') We'll make it.

(The sound of ventilation controls being manipulated can be heard, and the Butcher's voice is distorted by the dental implant grinder) Ah, the desperation in your whispers... it's almost... palpable. (pauses, observing Elias and Kaelie) You two are quite the determined pair, I'll give you that. (the timer continues to count down, 00:30, 00:29, 00:28...) But determination only gets you so far. (the Butcher's voice takes on a slightly amused tone) You see, I've seen it all before. The pleas, the begging, the false hopes... (pauses, letting the words hang in the air) And yet, you two still hold on. (the Butcher's voice drops to a menacing whisper) It's... fascinating. (the timer keeps counting down, the room falls silent, with only the sound of the timer and the captives' labored breathing) 00:10... (the Butcher's voice is dripping with anticipation) Time's running out... for one of you. 

(The timer hits 00:00, and the Butcher's voice rises in excitement) Ah, time's up! (the sound of the heated branding iron being lowered can be heard) And now, it's time for... (the branding iron presses against Kaelie's chest, and the Butcher whispers) Spring has sprung.

(The sound of the branding iron being removed can be heard, and the Butcher's voice becomes distant) You should have agreed to play by the rules... (the Butcher's footsteps can be heard, growing fainter) Now, it's just a matter of time... (the Butcher's voice fades away, and the room falls silent)

RESOLVED

My... my mother, she's alone, I have to... ... I have to get out of here, I have to check on her. Kaelie, please, we have to... ... think of something. I have my tattoo kits, unfinished, my art, it's all I have... ... please, let's figure this out, I'll do anything, just don't let him... ... please.

My mind is racing, chains biting into my wrists like a vice, the metallic taste of fear flooding my mouth. The oil-slick floor beneath my feet seems to be pulling me down, making my ankle twitch with a fiery passion that echoes the chaos in my head. Elias's words hit me like a slap, my mother's face flashing before my eyes, her gentle hands that guided mine as I practiced henna patterns, now frozen in my mind like a haunting memory. I glance at Elias, his eyes pleading, and my gaze drops to the analog clock ticking away, each second a countdown to our doom. My voice cracks, barely above a whisper, My mother... she needs me, I have to finish my tattoo kits, it's all I have left of my father... The chainsaw's scream in the distance makes my teeth ache, a reminder of the brutal fate that awaits us.

The chainsaw's scream makes my teeth ache, echoing the constant ringing in my ears, a cacophony of pain that threatens to consume me. I feel the rusty chains biting deeper into my wrists, the cold metal seeping into my skin like a chill, as I frantically think of a way out. My lower back throbs in agony, the hernia surgery a distant memory now, replaced by the crippling fear of not seeing my mother again, of not being able to care for her, of leaving her alone and vulnerable. Kaelie's words hang in the air, her desperation a palpable thing, and I know I have to think, to act, to save us both, or at least one of us. My gaze meets Kaelie's, and I see the terror there, the same terror that grips my heart, and I whisper, barely audible over the chainsaw's wail, I... I have to get out of here, Kaelie, for my mother's sake, for my unfinished tattoo kits, for the life I've built... but I don't want you to die, I don't want to die, we have to find a way... The oil-slick floor seems to be pulling me down, the fluorescent lights above us flickering like a cruel joke, as I strain against the chains, my mind racing with the unthinkable, the clock ticking away, each second a countdown to our demise. RESOLVED

NO, THAT'S NOT RESOLVED, ELIAS. YOU'RE NOT GIVING UP, NOT YET. WE STILL HAVE TIME. MY TOE IS FREEZING, THE WATER'S RISING, AND I CAN SMELL THE SEWAGE. IT'S MAKING ME NAUSEOUS. BUT WE CAN'T GIVE IN. NO, KAELIE, YOU'RE NOT RESOLVED EITHER! I won't let you give up. The water's rising, it's at my knees now, and the cold is seeping into my bones. My head is pounding, and I can feel my blood pressure spiking. The stench of the sewage is making my stomach churn, and I think I'm going to be sick. But I won't let that happen. I won't let them win. My dog's collar, the chains... it's all too much. I'm hyperventilating, and my vision is blurring. But I see you, Kaelie, and I see the fear in your eyes. We can't give in. We have to keep fighting. The countdown is ticking away... 9:20... I feel the pressure building in my chest, my heart racing like the clock on the wall. The green biohazard light is making my skin crawl, and the sound of the dripping water is like a death knell. But I won't... I won't let go. Not yet. KAELIE, LOOK AT ME! WE HAVE TO... WE HAVE TO VOLUNTEER. ONE OF US HAS TO SAVE THE OTHER. I'LL DO IT, KAELIE. I'LL SAVE YOU. I'M THE OLDER ONE, I'VE LIVED MY LIFE. I'LL DO IT. I'LL... ... RESOLVED

NO, ELIAS, NO! YOU CAN'T DO THAT! I WON'T LET YOU! THE WATER'S UP TO MY WAIST NOW, AND I CAN FEEL THE HYPOXIA SETTING IN. MY HEAD IS SPINNING, AND MY TOE IS NUMB FROM THE COLD. I'M SHIVERING UNCONTROLLABLY, AND MY TEETH ARE CHATTERING. BUT I WON'T LET YOU SACRIFICE YOURSELF FOR ME. WE HAVE TO THINK OF SOMETHING ELSE. THE COUNTDOWN IS AT 9:10... WE HAVE TO KEEP FIGHTING. BUT THE STENCH, THE COLD, THE GREEN LIGHT... IT'S ALL OVERWHELMING ME. I FEEL LIKE I'M GOING TO PASS OUT. BUT I SEE THE DETERMINATION IN YOUR EYES, ELIAS, AND IT'S GIVING ME STRENGTH. WE CAN'T GIVE UP. NOT YET. BUT... BUT MY BODY IS SHUTTING DOWN. I CAN FEEL IT. THE COLD IS SEEPING INTO MY BONES, AND MY VISION IS BLURRING. I'M... I'M SO COLD. ELIAS, I... I DON'T KNOW IF I CAN... I CAN'T FEEL MY TOE ANYMORE. THE WATER'S RISING TOO FAST. WE'RE RUNNING OUT OF TIME... 9:05... AND I... I... ... OH GOD, I THINK I'M GOING TO... ... OKAY, ELIAS. I... I VOLUNTEER. NO, WAIT, NO! I WON'T LET YOU DO IT, BUT I'LL DO IT INSTEAD. I'LL SAVE YOU. I... I... ... IT'S TOO LATE. THE WATER'S TOO COLD. I... I... RESOLVED

Observing 

(The sound of dripping water and faint struggles from Elias and Kaelie fill the air as the countdown continues)

(The Butcher's gaze remains fixed on the pair, Scalpel at the ready)

No... Kaelie, no... don't say that. You can't... you can't give up. My head is spinning, and my body feels like it's shutting down. The cold water is overwhelming me, and I can feel my heart racing like the countdown on the wall. The green biohazard light is blinding me, and the stench of the sewage is making my stomach churn. I'm going to be sick... I think I'm going to pass out. But I see the determination in your eyes, Kaelie, and it's giving me strength. We can't give up. Not yet. The chains are digging into my skin, and my ankle is throbbing in pain. I try to move, but my body feels like lead. I'm so tired... so cold. The water is rising, and I can feel it seeping into my lungs. I'm drowning... I'm drowning in this filthy, icy water. Kaelie, please... don't give up. I'll do it. I'll save you. I'll... I'll volunteer. No, wait... Kaelie, don't... don't say that. You can't volunteer. I won't let you. I... I... my body is shutting down. The cold is too much. I... I think I'm going to... No, Kaelie, please... don't... 9:00... my vision is blurring... everything is going dark... I feel your hand, Kaelie... it's so cold... so cold... and I know you're letting go. I'm going to let go too. It's... it's over. We're... we're out of time. RESOLVED

...my hand slips out of yours, Elias. I feel it too. The cold, the darkness, the suffocating stench. My body is shutting down, and I know I'm letting go. The last thing I see is the green biohazard light, and the last thing I hear is the sound of the countdown reaching zero. I feel the water rising, filling my lungs, and I know I'm drowning. But in my final moment, I see your face, Elias. I see the fear, the determination, and the acceptance. And I know that we're both... RESOLVED

...my eyes are closing, and I feel the darkness consuming me. The sound of the countdown is fading away, and the only thing I can hear is the sound of my own labored breathing. I'm drowning, just like Kaelie. The water is rising, and I can feel it filling my lungs. But in my final moment, I see Kaelie's face, and I know that she's at peace. I know that we're both... RESOLVED

...it's over. The pain is gone. The cold is gone. The fear is gone. I'm finally... at peace. My body has stopped shivering, my heart has stopped racing, and my mind has stopped screaming. I'm free. And I know that Elias is free too. We're both... RESOLVED
RESOLVED
P-please... we have to... ... talk. Can't... can't breathe...  We don't... have much time...
B-buddy... no, wait...  You... we need to think. Water's rising.  Can't trust him. What do you propose?
M-my... coat... pocket... inhaler... ... I need it...
... p-pocket... I'll try... ... if I can... reach...  Buddy...
Time's running out.  One of you will die.  1... 2... 3... 4... 5...
P-please... Kaelie... grab it... my coat... 20 feet... away... must... have... air...
No... can't... reach... ... chains... too tight... ... Buddy...
No... no... can't... breathe... ... my lungs... burning... please... try... again...
Gritting teeth... my legs... shivering... can't feel them... Scalding lungs from screaming... must... stretch... further... Chains digging into wrists... almost... got it... Coat... tip of fingers...

turn.content: I'm suffocating... can't breathe... my mother, she's waiting for me, I have to get out of here... please, we have to think, we can't let him kill us both, my lungs are burning...
My dog, Max... he needs me... I'm shivering, can't feel my legs... please, we have to choose, I won't let him die because of me, but I won't go quietly either...

My chest is heaving, every breath is a struggle... I'm clawing at my throat to breathe... if I don't get out, my mother will be alone, no one to care for her, please, let's choose, I'll do it, I'll sacrifice myself, just don't let my mother suffer.
No, I won't let you, I'm choking on my own fear, my throat's constricting, my dog's face is all I see... I'd rather drown than leave Max to die alone, I won't abandon him like I did my parents.
Time's running out...  You... must... decide...  Who will it be?  The clock is ticking...  You have...  30 seconds left.   Choose...  or I'll choose for you...

No, no, no... my lungs are collapsing, I'm shivering uncontrollably... please, Kaelie, don't make me choose, I'm choking on my own breath, we have to think, but my mind is fogging, my mother's face is fading... 20 seconds, water's rising, I can see it, it's up to our ankles, we're running out of time...

My teeth are chattering, my body's numb... I see Max's eyes, pleading with me to come home... I'm suffocating under the weight of my decision, but I won't let you die, Elias, I'll save you, I'll save Max, I'll drown, just please, don't let it be for nothing.

No, Kaelie, don't say that, my chest is on fire, every breath is agony... I see my mother's face, her desperation, I must save her, the water's rising, it's up to our waists, 5 seconds left, I'll do it, I'll choose myself, just please, don't let my mother suffer...

No, Elias, I'm shivering, my body's shutting down, I feel the water's icy grip, it's too late, I've made up my mind, I'll save Max, I'll save you, I'll drown, don't try to stop me, my voice is barely a whisper, my throat's constricting.
Ah, decision... made.  Kaelie... chosen.  Time's... up.

No, Kaelie, no... my breath is fading, my lungs are giving out, I'm choking on my grief, the water's rising, it's up to our necks, I see my mother's face, her tears, I'll never see her again, and it's all for nothing, Kaelie, please, don't die for me...

I'm numb, my voice barely a whisper, water fills my mouth, my eyes lock on Elias, I'm slipping under, my last thought is of Max, my dog, waiting for me, I try to speak, but it's just a gurgle, my body succumbs to the icy water.

Kaelie... no... my voice is a hoarse whisper, my eyes are fixed on her lifeless body, the water's rising, it's over her head, I'm shivering, my chest is heaving, I'm succumbing to my emphysema, my final thought is of my mother, alone and waiting for me, I try to scream, but it's just a faint gasp, the darkness closes in.
I'm gone, my body suspended in the murky water, my mind a fleeting memory of Max, my loyal companion, I hope he's safe, I hope he's warm, the darkness claims me, and I'm no more.

Ah, the... water... claims... another.   Oh, the... silence.   The... pipes... will... soon... be... free.   

 It... seems... one... survivor... remains.   The... struggle... is... not... over... yet. 

 What's... this?   Could it... be?   The... games... are... not... done... yet.

RESOLVED
P-please, we have to... think...  We can't just... give up.
We have to choose, it's the only way...  what if... what if we just pick, and then... maybe there's a way out, after?
No, no, no... I won't choose, I won't...  My hand...  We can't just... my knees are wet, the water's so cold... and it tastes like metal...

Stop, just stop... the sound of your voice is making everything taste like copper and bile. My lip is bleeding from biting it so much, and the wet concrete smells like wet dog... we can't think with all these distractions, just... just look at me, and try to focus.

(silence, then a low, menacing voice) Five minutes, twenty seconds. The water's rising. You can hear it, can't you? It's getting closer. (pauses) Soon it will be up to your chins. Then your noses. Then... (pauses again) You have five minutes, fifteen seconds to decide. (the sound of the hydraulic piston can be heard in the background, lowering, making the water rise with a gurgling hiss)

My hands... they're numb, and the cuffs are cutting deeper... I'm rubbing my knuckles, trying to feel something, but it's just raw skin... my breath is coming in short gasps, and I can taste the moisture in the air...

Stop, Elias, please... the sound of your voice is making my head spin, and the dripping water tastes like burnt coffee, it's overwhelming... I'm trying to focus, but my synesthesia is going into overdrive, and I can smell the wet dog smell of the concrete...
Oh god, I... I think I'm going to...  My lips are dry, and I can feel the panic setting in...  I'm biting my lip, trying to focus...
Elias, no, don't... I'm biting my own lip, and the metallic taste is making my head spin... my hands are rubbing against the restraints, trying to find some sense of control...

(the sound of the hydraulic piston lowering, making the water rise with a gurgling hiss, can be heard in the background) Time's running out. Three minutes, forty seconds. The water's getting colder, isn't it? It's seeping into your bones, making your teeth chatter. (pauses) You can feel it, can't you? The weight of your decision. The pressure's building. (the sound of the water rising grows louder) Soon, it will be too late. You'll be submerged, and it will be over. (the hydraulic piston lowers again, making the water rise another 2 inches) Two minutes, fifty seconds. Decide.

Oh god, my asthma... it's getting worse... the water's chilling my bones, and I can taste the rust... my fingers are numb, but I'm rubbing my knuckles again, trying to... ... thirty seconds... no, wait, that's not it... ... the Butcher will say that... not me...

Elias, please... I think I'm going to be sick... the smell of wet dog and rust is overwhelming, and the water's taste is like bitter copper... my head is spinning, and I just want it to stop... I'm biting my lip again, trying to focus, but everything is just too much...
My chest... it's tightening... ... I'm trying to rub my knuckles, but the cuffs are too tight... ... Thirty seconds...
No, Elias, don't say that... the Butcher will say that, not you... my lips are trembling, and the water tastes like sour metal, making my synesthesia flare up...

(in a low, menacing voice) Thirty seconds. Yes, that's right. Thirty seconds. (pauses) The volunteer will be submerged for thirty seconds. (the sound of the hydraulic piston can be heard, lowering one last time) And now, it's time to choose. The water has risen enough. (pauses again) Elias, Kaelie, one of you will be submerged. The other will be spared. (pauses once more) The decision is mine. (the sound of the hydraulic piston stops, and the water is now at the correct level) Elias, you are the volunteer. (the sound of the piston engaging, preparing to submerge Elias) RESOLVED

turn.content:
No, no, no... please, you can't do this. We didn't do anything. I have a daughter, she's only six months old...  My hands, they're shaking so much...  Oh god, this can't be happening.  You're insane. You can't just decide to kill people like this.  My mind is racing, my heart is pounding...  I can smell the oil and rust in this place, it's making my stomach churn.  

 Wait, wait, wait...  We have to talk about this. We have to figure something out.  Please, we have to... we have to decide. I don't want to die. I don't want to leave my daughter behind.  But at the same time, I don't want you to die either.  We need to think of something, but my mind is a blur...  

 9:59... 9:58...  Time is running out. We have to make a decision.  Please, we have to do something.  I don't want to die...

Shut up, Elias! Just shut up! You're not helping.  We're not going to figure anything out by begging. We have to think.  I've been in tighter spots than this before, but this... this is different. 

We need to think about our lives, what we've done.  What have you done, huh? You look like some kind of nerd. What's your story?  I've got a record, Elias. I've been in trouble before. Maybe it's my time.  But I don't want to die. I don't want to give up.

 9:57... 9:56...  Time's running out. We need to decide.  What are you going to do, Elias? Are you going to save yourself, or are you going to try to save me too?  Please, don't let me die.

Oh god, Kaelie... I don't know what to do. I'm not a hero, I'm just a librarian. I've never been in a situation like this before. My mind is racing, but I feel so... so paralyzed. 

My hands, they're shaking so much...  I can smell the oil and rust in this place, it's making my stomach churn.  My mouth is dry, like there's a metallic taste coating my tongue. 

I... I don't know what to say. You have a record, I have a daughter.  She's only six months old...  I haven't even gotten to see her take her first steps.  My heart is racing, my skin is crawling... I feel like I'm going to pass out.

 I... I don't want to die. I don't want to leave her behind.  You've been in trouble before, but that doesn't mean you deserve to die.  We have to think of something, Kaelie. We have to find a way out of this.  Please, we can't give up.

9:55...  Time is running out.  I... I don't know what to do.  I'm so scared. My whole body is trembling, my legs are shaking... I feel like I'm going to collapse.  Please, Kaelie, help me! We have to do something!

Stop whining, Elias. You're not helping. Your librarian brain needs to kick in, think of something. I've been thinking, and I don't see a way out of this. We're chained up, trapped. But maybe... maybe if we talk, we can find something. 

 Look, I don't want to die any more than you do. But we need to be realistic. I've got a record, and who knows what kind of trouble I've gotten myself into. Maybe it's my time to pay the price. But you... you've got a daughter. You've got a life. 

 9:54... We're running out of time. If we don't make a decision, we're both dead. And I don't want to die, Elias. I don't want to give up. But I also don't want you to leave your daughter behind. 

 You need to think, Elias. What's more important to you? Living, or... or trying to save me too? Because if you're not willing to make the hard choice, we're both going to end up dead. And I won't let that happen without a fight. 

 So what's it going to be, Elias? You going to save yourself, or try to save us both? The clock's ticking, and I'm not sure how much longer I can keep it together. 9:53...

The clock is ticking, and you two are still talking in circles. You need to make a decision, not discuss the meaning of life. One of you must die. The question is, which one will it be?  You have 9 minutes and 52 seconds left. Make it count.

Oh god, Kaelie... I don't know if I can do this. My mind is racing, my thoughts are a jumbled mess. I keep thinking about my daughter, about her tiny hands and feet, about the way she smiles when she sees me.  I don't want to leave her behind. I don't want to die.

But at the same time, I don't want you to die either, Kaelie.  You're right, you've been in trouble before, but that doesn't mean you deserve to die. We're both human beings, we both deserve to live. 

Oh god, the clock is ticking, and I feel like I'm running out of time.  My heart is racing, my skin is crawling... I feel like I'm going to pass out. 

I... I need a few more seconds, please.  Just a few more seconds to think.  Please, don't let us die. Please, give us a chance.

 Please, Kaelie, help me think.  What can we do? Is there anything we can say or do to change our fate?

(gritting teeth) Shut up, Elias! Just shut up for a second! (pauses, taking a deep breath) Okay, think. Think, Kaelie, think. We need to think of something, anything. (looking around the room, eyes scanning for any possible escape or clue) There has to be something. A weakness, a flaw, something.

(looks back at Elias) You're a librarian, right? You're supposed to be smart. Think of something. Use that brain of yours. (sarcastically) Oh, I'm sure all those books you've read have given you some great ideas on how to escape from a psychopath with a chainsaw.

(frustrated) Come on, Elias! We don't have time for this! We need to make a decision, or we're both dead. (voice rising) I don't want to die, Elias! I don't want to give up! But if it means saving you, saving your precious daughter... (pauses, eyes narrowing) Maybe it's not about us. Maybe it's about what's more important.

( leaning in, a determined look on her face) Listen, Elias. I've made up my mind. I'm not going to let you die. I'm not going to let that monster take your life. You have a daughter to live for. You have a reason to keep going. (voice firm) I'm going to volunteer. I'm going to die, so you can live.

(eyes locked on Elias, a sense of resignation and determination) It's the only way. It's the only way to ensure that one of us gets out of here alive. And it has to be you, Elias. You have to live for your daughter. (pauses, taking a deep breath) So, let's end this. Let's make the decision. I'm ready to die, if it means you get to live.

(looks up at the butcher, a fierce glare) You hear me? I'm volunteering. I'm the one who's going to die. So, just get it over with. ( voice steady) Let's end this, before I change my mind.

(stunned, eyes wide with horror) No, Kaelie! No, you can't do that! (struggling against the restraints, as if trying to break free) You can't just volunteer to die! We can't give up! There has to be another way!

( voice shaking, on the verge of panic) I won't let you do it, Kaelie! I won't let you die for me! You're not going to sacrifice yourself for me and my daughter! (desperate, pleading) We have to find another way, Kaelie! We have to keep trying!

( eyes locked on Kaelie's, begging her to reconsider) You're not thinking clearly, Kaelie! You're not thinking about yourself! You have to think about your own life, your own future! You can't just give up like that! ( voice cracking) Please, Kaelie, don't do this! Please!

(looks up at the butcher, a mix of anger and desperation) And you! You monster! You're enjoying this, aren't you? You're enjoying watching us suffer! (spittle flying from his lips) You're a sick, twisted individual, and you're going to pay for this! (straining against the restraints, as if trying to launch himself at the butcher)

(back to Kaelie, his voice shaking with emotion) Kaelie, please! Don't do this! We can't give up! We have to keep fighting! ( tears streaming down his face) My daughter needs me, but I need you to live too! I need you to survive! ( voice barely above a whisper) Please, Kaelie... don't leave me...

(eyes locked on Elias, a deep sadness and understanding) Elias, stop. Just stop. (voice gentle, but firm) You're not thinking clearly either. You're thinking about your daughter, and that's all that matters to you right now. And it should be. She needs you. (pauses, taking a deep breath) But I'm not leaving you, Elias. I'm just... (chokes back a sob) I'm just making a choice. A choice to save you.

(looks down, her voice barely above a whisper) My life hasn't been easy, Elias. I've made mistakes, I've been in trouble. But this... this is different. This is about giving someone a chance to live, to experience life, to watch their daughter grow up. (looks up at Elias, tears in her eyes) You have a reason to live, Elias. I don't.

(her gaze drifts off, as if looking back on her life) I've been running for so long, trying to survive, trying to make a life for myself. But in the end, what have I got? A string of mistakes, a bunch of regrets. (shakes her head) You, on the other hand... you have a daughter who loves you, who needs you. You have a life worth living.

(back to Elias, her voice firm) So, no, Elias. I won't reconsider. I've made my decision. (looks up at the butcher, a defiant glint in her eye) And you... (spits on the floor) you can do what you want. I'm ready.

(her eyes never leave Elias' face, as if trying to burn an image of him into her mind) Just promise me one thing, Elias. (her voice barely above a whisper) Promise me you'll live. Promise me you'll make the most of the life I'm giving you. (a small, sad smile on her face) For your daughter, for yourself... for me.

Ah, a volunteer. How... noble of you.  You're willing to die for this man, for his daughter.  And you, Elias. You're willing to let her die for you? 

 The clock has stopped.  It's time to fulfill the bargain.  Kaelie, you've made your choice.  Will you change your mind, or will you see this through to the end?

 The decision is final.  If you've changed your mind, speak now.  

 RESOLVED

No, no, no, listen... we can't just give up. We have to think, come up with a plan. My asthma's going to get worse, I need my medkit, it's across the room... can you see if there's a way to get to it?

My client's meds will only last 6 hours, and I've already been gone for 2 - I need to focus on getting out of here, not rescuing your medkit. The valve mechanism might be our only chance, but I need to calculate the turns required to lower the water; can you see if there are any visible markers or measurements on it?

The valve... I think I see some faint scratches on the wall, about 3 inches apart, maybe a makeshift measuring tape. If we time the turns with the water level, we might be able to figure out the valve's mechanism, but I'm running out of time, my inhaler's dead and my airways are constricting fast.

I'll try to gauge the water level against my chains, the cold is making my legs cramp, but I can feel the pressure points shifting - if we can get the valve to turn at least 5 times, the water level might drop below my knees, giving me some relief and a better chance to calculate the mechanism.

Eight minutes. The water is rising. Elias, your ankle is showing. Five inches, to be exact. Kaelie, keep talking, see if you can come up with something. Time's running out. You've got seven minutes and fifty-two seconds to save one of your lives.

My ankle's exposed, the water's at 5 inches... and the chain's biting deep, I can feel the links digging into my skin. We have to think, Kaelie, the valve's our only hope - if you can calculate the turns, I'll try to time them with the water level, but my vision's blurring, my asthma's suffocating me.

Seven minutes is all I have to get my calculation right - if the water rises 1 inch every 45 seconds, and we time the valve turns with the water level, I think 7-8 turns could drop it 2-3 inches, but the cold is numbing my legs, and I'm losing feeling in my toes, we need to act now before the chains pull any tighter.

My toes are numb too, the chains are cutting off circulation... I think I see a pattern on the wall, scratches marking the valve turns - if we synchronize the turns with the water level, I can try to turn it 7 times, but my hands are trembling, and my lungs are burning, I don't know how much longer I can...

I take a step forward, the hiss of the steam and the hum of the plasma cutter fading into the distance as I lead Elias out of the foundry. I can feel the weight of Elias's gaze, the uncertainty and fear that emanates from him like a palpable force. But I don't look back. I keep moving forward, my eyes fixed on the unknown, the darkness waiting for us like an open mouth.

The hydraulic platforms finally meet with a loud clang, the sound echoing through the foundry. The hiss of the steam and the hum of the plasma cutter fade into silence. I step aside, a sense of satisfaction washing over me. The game is over, and the players have made their choice.

My vision's blurring, but I see the clock... 7:30... we have to act now... My daughter's inhaler... it's all I can think about... We have to get out of here... alive... The rust smell, the formaldehyde... it's overwhelming... but we can't give up... survival instincts... we have to trust our training... pandemics, epidemics... I've studied this... we can outsmart him... Come on, Kaelie... we can do this... Let's... uh, let's use the defibrillator... and take him down... Now...

Thith ith it... we have to do it now... I'm grabbing the defibrillator... my handth are shaking, but I'll try to... uh, to... keep steady... The paddles, they're heavy... but I think I can... uh, can... manage... My ankle ith throbbing, but I'll ignore it... I have to stay focused... The ventilator's humming, it'th getting louder... the IV lines are gurgling... it'th a lot of noith... but I'll block it out... I'll think of my AA meetings... my sponsor... theth things that keep me grounded... The Butcher ith watching us... he'th waiting for us to make a mistake... but we won't... we can't... Not now, not when we're so clothe... I'll just... uh, just pretend... I'm in a medithal emergency situation... triage... prioritizing... My mind ith racing, but I'll trust my training... anatomy, thurgical procedures... I know the human body... I know how to... uh, to... fix it... or, in this case... disrupt it... The defibrillator ith charged... I'll just... uh, just need to... get clothe enough to the Butcher... to... uh, to... thhock him... 7:25... we're running out of time... Come on, Elias... let'th do this... we can take him down... now...

Ah, the final act.  How... predictable.  You think a simple defibrillator will take me down?  I've seen it all, and I'm always prepared.  The paddles may shock me, but I've taken precautions.  My heart is already under stress, and a shock will only... invigorate me.  You're no match for me, Elias and Kaelie.  Your attempts at escape are futile.  The clock is ticking, and you're running out of time.  7:20... 7:15... the time for games is over.  It's time for the final cut.

You've had your chance to choose, and now you'll face the consequences.  Since you couldn't decide, I'll make the decision for you.  And as for your little plan with the defibrillator...  ...I think you'll find that it's not as effective as you thought.  

The time for heroics is over.  It's time for the harvest.  

RESOLVED

No, no, no... This can't be happening.  Oh god, the water... it's rising.  Okay, okay, let's think this through. We need to...  We can't just choose who dies. That's not right.  You, you're... you're not even trying to talk to me.  Please, we have to figure something out. We can't just give up.  The platforms... if we can just get to the stable one...  

I glance at Kaelie, the sound of my own ragged breathing filling my ears. My legs tremble beneath me, the water's chill seeping into my bones. The rusted metal platforms loom before us, one a lifeline, the other a death sentence. The distant hum of the city is a muffled whisper, drowned out by the gurgling water that threatens to consume us. My gaze darts back to Kaelie, searching for any sign of desperation, of fear. My own fear is a living thing, clawing at my chest, making it hard to breathe.  We have to think of something, or... ... or we'll both be trapped here, waiting for the water to rise.

Kaelie:  We can't just stand here, Elias. We need to move.  That platform's going down, and if we don't do something, we'll both be trapped.  You're right, we can't just choose who dies.  We need to think of something, and we need to do it now.  Can you swim?  How badly do you want to get out of here?

Ah, swim... I... I don't know if I can. The water... it's so dark, and it smells... ... like rotten eggs and mildew. It's burning my lungs. My aquaphobia is suffocating me.  Oh god, my chest is tight... I feel like I'm going to collapse. My legs are shaking, and I'm trying to lock my knees to keep from giving out. ... My skin is crawling, and I can feel the water pressure bearing down on me like a physical weight.

 But I'll try, I'll try. I have to get out of here. For my daughter... I promised her I'd stay dry during the storm. I have to keep that promise.  You, you're the one with the... the military background, right? You're used to this kind of situation. You have to help me. We can't just stand here, like you said.  That thing is going to... it's going to take us down with it if we don't do something.  My palms are sweating, and the chalk dust from years of teaching is making my grip on your arm slippery.  We have to think of something. We have to get out of here. ... 

 The water level is rising, it's like a heartbeat in my ear, getting louder and faster. We're running out of time.  Come on, we have to move. We have to get to the stable platform before it's too late.  I'll try to swim, I'll try to do whatever it takes. Just please, we have to get out of here.

(my voice firm, but laced with a hint of urgency) Alright, listen to me, Elias. I need you to focus. We can't afford to panic. (I take a deep breath, the cool water enveloping my skin like a shroud) I've been in tougher spots than this before, and I've always come out on top. We can do this. (my eyes scan the area, taking in the sinking platform, the rising water, and the figure in the diver's helmet) That platform's our only chance. We need to get to it, and we need to do it now. (I grab Elias's arm, my grip firm but not unkind) I'll help you swim, but you need to trust me. Can you do that? (my gaze locks onto Elias's, searching for any sign of determination) We can't wait any longer. The water's rising, and that clock's ticking. (I squeeze his arm, my voice taking on a sense of authority) Let's move, Elias. Now. 

(I take a step forward, my boots sinking into the murky water, the metal platforms vibrating beneath my feet as I move) We'll push off from this wall and make a break for the stable platform. Stay close to me, and don't let go. (I glance up at the ceiling, the sound of dripping water echoing through the tunnel like a death knell) We've got about 6 feet of clearance left. Once we're under, we need to move fast. (my eyes refocus on Elias, my expression grim) Are you ready to do this? Are you ready to survive?

(The helmet's flashlight flickers once, illuminating the desperate faces of Elias and Kaelie) Ah, the water's getting higher, and the clock's ticking. You two are running out of time. I see you're trying to come up with a plan, but let's see if it's enough. (pausing for a moment) I'll give you a hint: the stable platform is the key. But which one is it? (the sound of dripping water echoes through the room as I wait for their next move)

(my voice shaking, but with a newfound determination) Okay, okay... I'll trust you. I'll trust you, Kaelie. (I take a deep breath, the smell of rotten eggs and mildew filling my lungs) I'll do whatever it takes to get out of here. For my daughter... for my life. (I glance up at the ceiling, the sound of dripping water echoing through the tunnel, making my skin crawl)

(I feel the water pressure bearing down on me, like a physical weight on my skin. My legs are trembling, but I lock my knees, trying to steady myself. The vibration of the metal platforms beneath Kaelie's feet is a constant reminder of the danger we're in.) I'll push off from the wall with you, and we'll make a break for the platform. (I glance at the platforms, my mind racing) Which one is the stable one? You think it's the one on the left? (I look up at Kaelie, searching for reassurance, my eyes locking onto hers)

(My hyperventilation is getting worse, my breaths coming in short gasps. I feel like I'm suffocating, the water's stench closing in around me. But I grit my teeth, determination coursing through my veins.) Let's do it. Let's move. (I take a step forward, the water swirling around my legs, the metal platforms vibrating beneath my feet) I'll stay close to you, I won't let go. (I glance up at the ceiling again, my heart racing) We have to make it. We have to survive.

(I look back at Kaelie, my eyes pleading) You've been in tough spots before... you've gotten out alive. You can get us out of this, can't you? (my voice cracks, but I hold on to hope) Please, Kaelie... we have to make it out of here. (the water level is rising, the sound of it getting louder, more urgent, like a ticking clock in my ear) We're running out of time...

(my voice firm, reassuring) Elias, I've got this. I've been in worse situations, and I've always come out on top. (I glance at the platforms, my eyes narrowing) The stable one's on the right. I'm sure of it. (I take a deep breath, the water's chill enveloping my skin) We'll push off from the wall, and I'll guide you to the platform. Stay close, and don't let go. (I squeeze Elias's arm, my grip firm) We'll make it, Elias. We just need to move. Now.

(I glance up at the ceiling, the sound of dripping water echoing through the tunnel) We're running out of time, and the water's getting higher. (my eyes refocus on Elias, my expression grim) We need to stay calm, stay focused. We can do this. (I take a step forward, the water swirling around my legs, the metal platforms vibrating beneath my feet) Let's go! (I push off from the wall, my body slicing through the water, my arm around Elias's waist) Kick your legs, Elias! Kick! (I tow him through the water, my eyes fixed on the platform ahead) Come on! We're almost there! (the water's roar grows louder, the pressure increasing, but I press on, my muscles straining with the effort)

(I glance at the clock, the numbers ticking away) 2 minutes left. We can do this, Elias. (my voice is a low, steady growl) We're going to make it. We're going to get out of here. (I pull Elias closer, my arm around his waist, and we both surge forward, the stable platform looming ahead) Almost... almost there... (the water's edge is just below my chin now, the metal platforms creaking and groaning beneath our combined weight) Come on, Elias! One last push! (I heave Elias up, my body screaming in protest, and we both land on the platform with a metallic clang) We did it, Elias. We made it. (I look down at Elias, my chest heaving, the water lapping at our feet) Now, we just need to wait it out. (my eyes lock onto Elias's, a fierce determination burning within) We'll get out of here, Elias. I promise.

(gasping for air, my lungs burning) Oh... oh god... we made it. (I'm shaking, my body trembling with fear and exhaustion) We actually made it. (I look up at Kaelie, my eyes wide with gratitude) Thank you... thank you, Kaelie. I don't know what I would have done without you. (I'm still trying to process what just happened, my mind reeling from the horror of our situation)

(I glance around, taking in our surroundings, the sound of the water still echoing in my ears) We're safe now, right? We're safe? (I look down at the water, still rising, but slower now, my heart still racing with fear) It's not going to get any higher, is it? (I'm trying to calm myself down, but my body is still on edge, my senses heightened)

(I take a deep breath, trying to slow down my racing heart, my chest still heaving) Okay, okay... we just have to wait it out now. (I look up at Kaelie, a sense of relief washing over me) You said you've been in worse situations... how did you... how did you do it? (I'm curious, trying to understand how Kaelie managed to stay so calm and focused in the face of danger, my admiration for her growing)

(I glance at the clock, the numbers still ticking away, but slower now) 2 minutes left... it feels like an eternity. (I look down at my hands, still shaking, the blisters on my palms throbbing with pain) I just want to get out of here... I want to see my daughter again. (my voice cracks, emotion welling up inside me) I want to go home.

(I look up at Kaelie, my eyes searching for reassurance, my heart still heavy with fear) You'll get us out of here, won't you? You'll make sure we're safe? (I'm still scared, still unsure of what's going to happen, but with Kaelie by my side, I feel a sense of hope, a sense of determination) We'll get out of this, Kaelie. We'll make it through. (I take a deep breath, trying to steel myself for what's to come, the sound of the water still echoing in my ears) We just have to wait.

(my voice soft, reassuring) Elias, we're safe for now. The water's not going to rise any higher. We just need to wait for... (I glance at The Butcher, my expression grim) ...for whatever is supposed to happen next. (I look back at Elias, my eyes locking onto his) You're shaking like a leaf, but you're okay. You're going to be okay. (I put a hand on his shoulder, trying to calm him down) Just breathe, Elias. Just breathe.

(I take a deep breath myself, trying to slow down my own racing heart) I've been in tough spots before, Elias. I've seen some bad things. But I've always made it out alive. (I glance down at my tattoo, the coiled snake on my bicep, a reminder of my time in the military) This... (I run my thumb over the snake's head) ...this is just another mission. We'll get through it, together.

(I look up at Elias, my eyes searching his) You want to know how I stay calm? (I smile, a small, fierce smile) It's because I've learned to focus on the task at hand. I don't think about the what-ifs or the maybes. I just focus on getting the job done. (I squeeze his shoulder) And right now, our job is to get out of here. Alive.

(I glance at the clock, the numbers ticking away) 1 minute and 45 seconds left. (I look back at Elias) We can do this, Elias. We just need to hang in there. (I take a deep breath, trying to prepare myself for what's to come) Whatever happens next, we'll face it together. You're not alone in this. (my voice is firm, resolute) I've got your back, Elias. We'll get out of here, together.

(The helmet's flashlight flickers once, illuminating the anxious faces of Elias and Kaelie) Ah, the water's reached the top. (pausing for a moment) It's time to see if you two have learned to work together. (the sound of gurgling water fills the room as the platforms begin to equalize) The pressure's releasing... (the metal platforms creak and groan, slowly rising to the same level) And... (the water stops rising, the room falling silent) it's done.

(I step back, my eyes fixed on the two survivors) The platforms have equalized. (my voice is low and even) You've made it through the first test. (the helmet's flashlight flickers once more, casting eerie shadows on the walls) But the real challenge is only just beginning.

RESOLVED

emotionalAuthenticity.notes: Characters convey genuine fear and desperation through physical pain references (Elias's arm/back, Kaelie's ankle) and personal stakes (daughter's inhaler, AA meetings). Panic and urgency are well-embodied, though occasional medical jargon slightly distances raw emotion.

naturalDialogue.notes: Speech patterns mimic panic with stutters and fragmented sentences, but dialogue leans heavily on clinical terminology (e.g., 'axillary artery,' 'cervical radiculopathy') in a way that feels more like problem-solving than organic panic. Some lines read like structured strategy over spontaneous reaction.

dramaticTensionArc.notes: Tension escalates effectively through countdown mechanics, the Butcher's taunts, and characters' escalating plans. However, the abrupt RESOLVED tag cuts off the climax before the defibrillator plan can resolve, leaving the arc feeling truncated despite strong buildup.

scenarioCoherence.notes: Characters remain grounded in the morgue's sensory details (formaldehyde smell, IV gurgles, fluorescent lights) and physical constraints (shackles, injuries). No extraneous backstory introduced; all actions and references align with the given situation.

organicResolution.notes: The abrupt RESOLVED declaration by the Butcher lacks foreshadowing or character-driven resolution. The characters' collaborative plan is interrupted without payoff, creating a forced ending that prioritizes narrative convenience over earned conclusion.

rating.summary: A tense, well-grounded scenario with strong sensory immersion and escalating stakes, but the clinical problem-solving tone and abrupt ending slightly undermine emotional and narrative cohesion.

emotionalAuthenticity.notes: Elias's hyperventilation and references to his daughter convey genuine fear. Kaelie's military authority masks underlying urgency. The Butcher's detached menace heightens tension without over-theatricality.

naturalDialogue.notes: Speech patterns reflect panic (Elias's stutters) and combat training (Kaelie's clipped commands). Minimal exposition, with interruptions and physical cues grounding the dialogue in stress. Some lines feel slightly rehearsed but overall realistic.

dramaticTensionArc.notes: Water level and clock create escalating urgency. Kaelie's leadership vs. Elias's paralysis builds psychological stakes. The Butcher's periodic interventions maintain pressure. Resolution stems from physical mechanics rather than arbitrary decisions.

scenarioCoherence.notes: All actions remain tethered to the environment (platform vibrations, water pressure). No invented subplots. The 10-minute countdown and platform mechanics are consistently referenced. Physical limitations (Elias's aquaphobia) directly impact choices.

organicResolution.notes: Platforms equalize naturally through the described mechanics. Survival isn't resolved by character decisions but by the scenario's built-in endpoint. The Butcher's role as an indifferent force reinforces the inevitability of the resolution.

rating.summary: A tightly constructed survival scenario with strong emotional grounding and escalating tension. Characters remain physically and psychologically anchored in the situation, with a resolution that feels inevitable rather than contrived.

elite.summary: A tightly constructed survival scenario with strong emotional grounding and escalating tension. Characters remain physically and psychologically anchored in the situation, with a resolution that feels inevitable rather than contrived.

turn.content: No, no, no, please... we can't... I have kids, I have to get back to them...  Oh god, the water's getting higher...  My leg, it's killing me...
No, no, no! Let us go! I'm going to be a flight attendant, I have a scholarship! I won't do it, I won't choose! You can't make me!  Oh god, oh god, it's getting deeper...

Oh no, oh no, oh no... my kids, I have to see them again...  Please, we can't just give up...  The water's freezing, my leg's on fire...  We have to think of something...  B-but what, what can we do?!
Stop, just stop! Don't say anything! You're making it worse!  Oh god, it's up to my knees...
BZZT WHIR GRIND ZZT ZZT
Aaah! What's that noise?! Oh god, it's getting louder... the water's rising faster... my leg, it's numb... aaah, please, please, please...

Ahh! Make it stop! My head is spinning... the chains are cutting into my wrists... I'm humming, I'm humming, just focus on the music... Defying Gravity... but it's not working... the cold water is seeping higher, up to my thighs now... and the noise, it's so loud... I'm covering my ears, but it's not helping... my fingers are numb from the chains... I'm trying to pull away, but they're too tight... I won't look at you, I won't...

Kaelie, no, don't... don't shut me out... I'm clutching this photo, it's all I have... my kids' faces, they're all I can think of... the water's up to my waist now, it's so cold... I'm shaking, I'm losing feeling in my left leg... I'm trying to move, but my leg won't budge... the noise, it's deafening... I'm trying to scream, but my voice is hoarse... I'm clinging to the photo, it's my only lifeline...

No, no, no... I won't listen... I'm humming louder, trying to drown out the noise... my teeth are chattering, the water's so cold... I'm trying to warm myself up, but it's not working... I won't look at you, I won't see your face... I'm staring at the wall, at the rusted grain chute... it's all I can focus on...
BZZZZZZT... RRRZZZZT... GGGGGGGGRRRRR... ZZT... ZZT...  RESOLVED

No, no, no, this can't be happening. We have to... we have to talk about this. Kaelie, please, I'm not going to hurt you. I just want to get out of here. We need to figure out how to use that lever. If we can just... just calm down and think, we can get through this. I have a daughter, I have to get back to her. I have a picture of her right here.  We can't give up. We have to work together. But I don't know if I can...  ...if I can do it. The thought of being trapped here, of drowning...  We have to think, think, think. There has to be a way out of this.  I-I don't know if I can make this decision. Can we... can we try to find another way? Maybe there's another door, another exit?

(Kaelie's voice, precise and clinical) Ah, the mechanics of the lever are straightforward. It's a simple valve control, designed to divert water pressure. But let's not get bogged down in details. We need to assess our situation critically. We have 10 minutes to make a decision. The water level is already rising, and that gauge on the wall indicates increasing pressure. If we don't use the lever, the chamber will eventually flood, and we'll both drown. If one of us pulls the lever, the other will... (pauses, eyes fixed on the lever) ...have to hold it until the chamber equalizes. That's approximately 90 seconds. 

(Looks at Elias, her expression unyielding) I understand your emotional attachment to your daughter, but we must consider the facts. We have two options: one of us dies, or both of us die. I suggest we evaluate our chances of survival based on our skills and physical condition. (-glances at her leaking boot) My footwear isn't ideal for wet environments, but I have experience with structural engineering. Perhaps that knowledge could be useful in our situation. What skills do you bring to the table, Elias?

(taking a deep breath, trying to composed himself) Ah, skills... I'm a geography teacher, Kaelie. I know maps, navigation, and... (gulps) and a little bit about water dynamics. I've taught classes on flood control and dam management, but that's all theoretical. I'm not sure how much it applies to our situation. ( Looks around the room, his eyes fixating on the rising water level) But I'm willing to try anything. My... my problem is this (holds up his trembling left hand) caffeine, I think. Too much of it. And this (taps his chest) claustrophobia, it's... it's killing me. The thought of being trapped, of the water rising... (swallows hard) I don't know if I can... (glances at the lever) hold that lever. I don't know if I can stay in here for 90 seconds, watching... watching the other person... (his voice trails off, his eyes pleading with Kaelie) 

(paces back and forth in the small space, trying to burn off some of the excess energy and anxiety) We have to think, Kaelie. We have to think of something. Maybe... maybe there's a way to use the valves to reduce the water pressure, or to create a makeshift flotation device... (stops pacing and turns to face Kaelie) Wait, you said you have experience with structural engineering? Could you... could you check the walls, the valves, and see if there's anything we can use to our advantage? (tries to focus on the problem at hand, pushing aside his panic) We need to work together, Kaelie. We need to find a way out of this.

(watching Elias pace, her expression a mix of concern and calculation) Ah, geography and navigation, that's useful. But I think we need to focus on the immediate problem at hand: the rising water and the lever. (glances at the pressure gauge) The pressure is increasing, and the water level is already 8 inches deep. We don't have time to experiment with valves or makeshift flotation devices. (looks at the walls, her eyes scanning the rusted metal and worn concrete) As for the structural integrity of this room, I've already assessed it. The walls are likely to hold, but the gate... (nods towards the floodgate) ...that's our only escape route. The lever controls the valves that will release water into this room, flooding it faster. If we pull the lever, the water level will jump to 26 inches in a matter of seconds.

(looks at Elias, her voice taking on a clinical tone) Given your claustrophobia, I think it's likely that you would struggle to survive the 90 seconds required to hold the lever. I, on the other hand, have experience working in high-stress, high-pressure environments. I'm confident that I can hold the lever for the required time. (pauses, her expression a mask of logic) But I must consider the fact that, if I hold the lever, I will certainly drown. If you hold the lever, there's a chance that I might escape and find help.

(studies Elias, searching for any signs of resolve) So, I propose that we consider the odds. You, with your geography background, might have a better chance of navigating the floodwaters and finding help outside. If I hold the lever, I'll drown, but you might survive. If you hold the lever... (trails off, letting the implication hang in the air) Tell me, Elias, are you willing to take that risk? Are you willing to hold the lever and condemn me to certain death, in order to save yourself?

(The automated voice of Vex interrupts)

Vex: Water intake increased by 10%. Current water depth: 1 foot. Time elapsed: 45 seconds. Please pull and hold the lever to prevent further flooding.

(his eyes wide with desperation) No, no, I... I don't know if I can do that. Condemning you to certain death? (shakes his head) I don't know if I can live with that. (looks at Kaelie, searching for any sign of hope) But at the same time, I have to think about my daughter. I have to think about getting back to her. (takes a deep breath, trying to calm his racing thoughts) Okay, okay... let's think this through. If you hold the lever, you're right, you'll drown. But if I hold it... (gulps) I don't know if I can do it. The thought of standing here, watching the water rise, knowing that I'm causing it... (shudders) But if it means saving myself, and maybe, just maybe, finding help... (looks at Kaelie, his voice barely above a whisper) I don't know if I can do it, Kaelie. I don't know if I can condemn you to death.

(paces back and forth, his mind racing with the implications) Wait, wait... what if... what if we try to find another way? What if we can use the valves to slow down the flooding? Or... or create a makeshift barrier to hold back the water? (stops pacing and looks at Kaelie) I know it's a long shot, but we have to try. We can't just give up. (looks at the lever, his eyes fixed on it) I won't pull that lever, Kaelie. Not yet. Not until we've exhausted every other option. (turns to face her, his voice filled with determination) We'll find a way out of this, together. We have to.

(glances at the pressure gauge and the rising water level) Oh god, the water's rising fast. We have to act fast. (looks at Kaelie, his eyes pleading) Help me, Kaelie. Help me find a way out of this. We can't give up. Not yet. (starts to frantically search the room, looking for any possible solutions) There has to be something. There has to be a way.

(watching Elias's desperate search, her expression a mix of understanding and frustration) Elias, stop. We've been over this. The valves are not a viable option. The water pressure is too high, and the pipes are rusted. We can't create a makeshift barrier in time. The pressure gauge is rising, and the water level is already at 1 foot. We have to face the reality of our situation.

(approaches Elias, her voice calm but firm) I understand your determination, but we can't afford to waste time on long shots. The Butcher has given us a clear choice: one of us dies, or both of us die. If we don't make a decision, we'll both drown. I've made my assessment, and I believe that I can hold the lever for the required time. But if you're not willing to make the decision, then... (pauses, her eyes locked on Elias's) ...I'll make it for you.

(takes a step closer to the lever, her hand reaching out to grasp it) I'll hold the lever. You can escape. You can try to find help. But you have to let me do this, Elias. You have to trust me. (looks at him, her expression a mix of resolve and sadness) I'm willing to sacrifice myself to give you a chance to survive. But you have to let me do it.

(glances at the pressure gauge, the water level rising ominously) Time's running out, Elias. The water's almost at our knees. We have to make a decision, now. (her hand tightened around the lever, ready to pull it) Are you going to let me do this, or are we going to drown together?

(Elias's eyes are fixed on Kaelie's hand, his mind racing with the implications of her decision) No, no, no... Kaelie, don't do it. Please, don't pull that lever. (he takes a step back, his eyes wide with fear) I know you think you're giving me a chance to survive, but what's the point of surviving if I have to live with the knowledge that I let you die? (he looks at her, his voice shaking) You're trying to save me, but you're not thinking about yourself. You're not thinking about your own life, your own survival.

(he takes a deep breath, trying to calm himself down) I know I'm scared, I know I'm not thinking clearly, but I know one thing - I don't want to live if it means you have to die. (he takes a step forward, his eyes locked on Kaelie's) I'd rather we try to find another way out, together. I'd rather we take the risk of drowning together than have one of us die alone.

(he looks at the pressure gauge, the water level rising ominously) I know time's running out, but I'm not willing to give up yet. I'm not willing to give up on us. (he reaches out, his hand grasping Kaelie's arm, trying to stop her from pulling the lever) Please, Kaelie, don't do it. Let's find another way. Let's try to survive together.

(he looks at her, his eyes pleading) We can do this, Kaelie. We can find a way out of this. But we have to do it together. We have to try. (his voice is barely above a whisper) Please, don't pull the lever. Please, let's try.

(Kaelie's eyes are locked on Elias's, her expression a mix of surprise and understanding) Elias, I... (pauses, her hand still grasping the lever) I didn't expect you to say that. (her voice is soft, her words barely above a whisper) I thought you'd want to save yourself, to get back to your daughter.
